rulings.Case Title: PAUL GREENFIELD VS. SAN FRANCISCO BAY CONSERVATION AND DEVELOPMENT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 3, 2023 line 1. RESPONDENT SAN FRANCISCO BAY CONSERVATION AND DEVELOPMENT, LAWRENCE GOLDZBAND DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION is SUSTAINED without leave to amend. Leslie Salt Co. v. San Francisco Bay Conservation & Development Commission (1984) 153 Cal.App.3d 605 =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: CHL INTERNATIONAL ASSOCIATES, A CALIFORNIA VS. CHITO KUMAR SAHA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Cross-Defendant Vikrant Punj And His Counsel And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 3, 2023 line 2. DEFENDANT CHITO SAHA NOTICE OF MOTION TO COMPEL CROSS-DEFENDANT VIKRANT PUNJ AND HIS COUNSEL AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Cross-Defendant Punj and his counsel shall return (1) documents contained in Ex. A Bates stamped CS000001-10; and (2) the e-mail from Tour- Sarkissian forwarding communications with Nerland to Saha contained within Ex. C as previously ordered. The Court finds that the following documents are not privileged and are not subject to return: (1) communications between Tour-Sarkissian and Nerland contained in the remainder of Ex. C; and (2) documents contained in Ex. B. Saha was provided 2 opportunities to present its argument regarding privilege including 2 court-ordered opportunities to provide a privilege log. Such privilege log regarding Ex. B does not support privilege. See entries regarding Ex. B (Sender: Chito Saha; Recipient: Christine Tour-Sarkissian; Description: E-mail from Christine Tour-Sarkissian to Chito Saha regarding representations on Saha) where Tour-Sarkissian is claimed to be the recipient of the e-mail from Tour-Sarkissian. Request for sanctions is denied =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: PHUONG T. NGUYEN VS. LONDON BREED ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside And Vacate, Or In The Alternative To Correct The Order On Defendans Demurrer To Second Amended Complaint Filed By Mayor London Breed, Sheriff Paul Miyamoto, Tina Tam, Michael Borovina, The San Francisco Public Utilities Commission, Jennifer Choi And Corey
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 3, 2023 line 3. PLAINTIFF PHUONG NGUYEN NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO SET ASIDE AND VACATE, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE TO CORRECT THE ORDER ON DEFENDANS DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY MAYOR LONDON BREED, SHERIFF PAUL MIYAMOTO, TINA TAM, MICHAEL BOROVINA, THE SAN FRANCISCO PUBLIC UTILITIES COMMISSION, JENNIFER CHOI AND COREY is DENIED. Moving party provides no basis for vacating September 15, 2023 order sustaining the demurer to the 14th cause of action without leave to amend. The Court's tentative ruling issued on September 14, 2023 became the order of the Court on September 15, 2023, having not been contested by either party. The prevailing party presented an order tracking the tentative ruling for execution in open court. The order was therefore executed and entered. The action shall proceed in accordance with the Code of Civil Procedure on the Second Amended Complaint as it stands in light of September 15, 2023 order. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JOHN E. WAI VS. FATHI SAID ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Modify Tro And Appoint A Limited Purpose Receiver And For Osc Re: Contempt
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 3, 2023 line 4. PLAINTIFF FELIX WAI, TWO TO FOUR, LLC, MFR APARTMENTS, LLC, GREEN HILLS VIEWS, LLC Notice Of Motion And Motion To Modify Tro And Appoint A Limited Purpose Receiver And For Osc Re: Contempt is continued to January 24, 2023. Based on the procedural history of this case it appears that parties have made attempts to resolve the timely rent payment issue, but so far have not been successful. The Court is not ready at this time to impose an expensive receivership where a less expensive measure may be appropriate and would provide a resolution. No later than noon on the 13th of November 2023; 11th of December 2023 and 10th of January 2024) tenants to deliver a check payable to the landlord to the office of tenant's counsel. Tenant's counsel to make the check available for pick up between 1 p.m. and 5 p.m. on the same date. (The parties may fashion a mutually agreeable method of verifiable electronic transfer). No later than 2 business days thereafter tenants to file a declaration attesting to delivery of the payment. In the event the payment is not timely available, Plaintiff may appear ex-parte to advance the hearing date on this motion. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
